title: Standard Template Library
description: Explore STL containers like std::vector and std::unordered_map, and understand their performance implications in games.
learningObjectives: Use std::vector dynamically and understand capacity vs size
Use std::unordered_map for O(1) key-value hash lookups
Analyze the cache and performance impact of collections during game updates

sections.title: Dynamic Containers and Allocation Costs
sections.content:
The Standard Template Library (STL) provides dynamic collections. In games, `std::vector` is the absolute standard because it stores elements contiguously. However, pushing elements dynamically past capacity triggers a heap re-allocation and copy of the entire collection. Pre-allocating memory using `.reserve(N)` is crucial to maintain steady frame rates. 

For key-value pairs, `std::unordered_map` offers O(1) lookups via hash functions but incurs a heavy cache penalty due to node-based heap configurations.

sections.title: Optimizing Vector Allocations
